steps.name: AllocStatusRow V1->V2 rkyv envelope (additive workload_addr)
steps.description: Add AllocStatusRowEnvelope::V2 carrying workload_addr: Option<Ipv4Addr> per the development.md rkyv 6-step version-bump procedure (D-BLOCKER2). This is the persisted observation surface the BackendDiscoveryBridge hydrates the per-alloc canonical address from; persisting the materialized slot*base-at-provision addr (not the NetSlot to recompute) keeps install/observe/advertise byte-identical.
criteria: S-V2: the existing FIXTURE_V1 golden bytes decode through AllocStatusRowEnvelope::into_latest() to an AllocStatusRowV2 whose workload_addr is None (additive From<AllocStatusRowV1> for AllocStatusRowV2 defaults the new field absent); every other field equals the canonical V1 payload byte-for-byte.
S-V2: an AllocStatusRowV2 carrying Some(Ipv4Addr) round-trips archive -> access -> deserialize -> into_latest() equal to the original.

Envelope shape complete per the 6-step: V2 appended to the enum (V1 discriminant unmoved), pub type AllocStatusRow re-aliased to AllocStatusRowV2, pub type AllocStatusRowLatest = AllocStatusRowV2, latest(p) -> Self::V2(p), From<V1> for V2 additive (workload_addr: None), into_latest() chains V1 => Ok(v1.into()) / V2 => Ok(v2).

Golden discipline held: FIXTURE_V1 is NOT touched; FIXTURE_V2 added in the same commit; GOLDEN_DISCRIMINANT_OFFSET_V1 / discriminant_offset_from_end() re-pinned via the triangulation test (adding Option<Ipv4Addr> = 4 bytes behind the Option discriminant shifts the trailing root footprint; the offset is empirical).

Obligation #5: AllocStatusRowV2.workload_addr carries a rustdoc comment naming it a materialized slot x base-at-provision join (a frozen snapshot whose inputs are immutable except under redeploy) and stating the #239 Phase-1 single-cut constraint: a base change is a full redeploy / re-provision / re-observe, NOT a live re-tune.
Mutation gate >=80% on the V2 codec surface (latest / From<V1> / into_latest projection) via cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests (Lima-routed on macOS).
steps.implementation_notes: Rust test paradigm: this is a schema-evolution golden-fixture step (.claude/rules/testing.md 'Archive schema-evolution roundtrip', mandatory call site). UNSKIP the two committed RED scaffolds (#[should_panic(expected="RED scaffold")], bb63d878) -- replace each panic body with real assertions and add the production API the envelope needs. Default-lane (no integration-tests feature, pure in-process rkyv, no I/O). proptest is eligible for the V2 Some(addr) round-trip arm (generate an arbitrary Ipv4Addr -> archive -> access -> deserialize -> assert eq); the V1 arm is example-pinned (the golden FIXTURE_V1 bytes ARE the pinned example). Follow development.md 'rkyv schema evolution' -> 'Version-bump procedure' steps 1-6 in a SINGLE commit. Mutation-gate target = the V2 codec (latest / From<V1> for V2 / into_latest) -- a mutant flipping the additive None default or dropping a match arm must be caught.

steps.name: AllocationSpec fields + C3 injection + listen-port projection + exit-observer copy
steps.description: Add the pure in-memory per-alloc channel for the canonical address and declared service ports (D-A1 plumbing). Set workload_addr at the C3 provision seam off plan.workload_addr (same channel as netns/host_veth, no serde/rkyv); project the declared Service listener ports via a new WorkloadLifecycle::project_service_listen_ports mirroring project_probe_descriptors; copy spec.workload_addr into the written AllocStatusRowV2 in the exit-observer write path.
criteria: AllocationSpec gains pub workload_addr: Option<Ipv4Addr> and pub service_ports: Vec<NonZeroU16> (pure in-memory; derives Debug/Clone/PartialEq/Eq only -- NO serde, NO rkyv -- symmetric with the existing netns/host_veth slot-derived channels). Both default None/empty for host-netns workloads (every current fixture).
C3 seam: provision_and_inject_netns sets spec.workload_addr = Some(plan.workload_addr) beside the existing spec.netns / spec.host_veth injection, off the same plan (action_shim/mod.rs:792-824).

New WorkloadLifecycle::project_service_listen_ports(intent) -> Vec<NonZeroU16>: Service(svc) => svc.listeners.iter().map(|l| l.port).collect(); Job/Schedule => Vec::new() (mirrors project_probe_descriptors at workload_lifecycle.rs:1078-1094). Unit-tested: a Service projects its listener ports; a Job and a Schedule each project the empty vec.

service_ports threaded into the emitted AllocationSpec.service_ports at the IDENTICAL hydrate-desired site/shape as probe_descriptors (reconciler_runtime.rs:2317) -- Obligation #2b: pin this seam, do NOT improvise a new site or a new ObservationStore method.
Exit-observer / alloc-status write path copies spec.workload_addr into the written AllocStatusRowV2.workload_addr (an observed input: the addr the node provisioned this alloc into).
Mutation gate >=80% on project_service_listen_ports (the Service-arm vs Job/Schedule-empty-arm partition is a canonical mutation surface).
steps.implementation_notes: Rust test paradigm: Tier-1 DST / default-lane unit. project_service_listen_ports is a pure function -- unit-test the three kind arms directly (Service -> ports, Job -> empty, Schedule -> empty); proptest is eligible over an arbitrary non-empty NonZeroU16 listener set asserting the projected set equals the declared set (this is the producer side of S-PORTSET, finalized in 02-01). Per CLAUDE.md 'implement to the design': add ONLY the two AllocationSpec fields + the one projection fn + the named injection assignments -- do NOT invent a new spec type, a new ObservationStore method, or a builder override (the dependency must be set at the C3 site, not defaulted). The exit-observer copy is an observed-input write, not a derived value. Depends on 01-01 for the AllocStatusRowV2.workload_addr field the exit-observer writes.

steps.name: Bridge advertises workload_addr:port + RunningAllocSet Set->Map + hydrate_actual
steps.description: Flip the BackendDiscoveryBridge advertise address from host_ipv4 to the canonical per-workload workload_addr when present (D-B2), widen RunningAllocSet.running from BTreeSet to BTreeMap<AllocationId, Option<Ipv4Addr>> to carry the per-alloc addr, and populate that map in hydrate_actual from the V2 observation row. ServiceBackendRow.vip is UNCHANGED (dialable-VIP is #61 territory).
criteria: S-BRIDGE (Some arm): when actual.running[alloc] is Some(workload_addr), the emitted Backend.addr == workload_addr:listener_port; the emitted service vip is UNCHANGED.

S-BRIDGE (None arm, error/edge): when actual.running[alloc] is None (host-netns / non-Path-A alloc), the emitted Backend.addr == host_ipv4:listener_port (fallback behaviour UNCHANGED); the emitted service vip is UNCHANGED.

RunningAllocSet.running widened BTreeSet<AllocationId> -> BTreeMap<AllocationId, Option<Ipv4Addr>> (BTreeMap not HashMap -- the bridge fingerprints the iterated backend set deterministically, per development.md 'Ordered-collection choice').

Obligation #2a: hydrate_actual populates RunningAllocSet.running from each Running V2 alloc-status row's workload_addr (pin WHERE the per-alloc workload_addr is read into the map; obs.alloc_status_rows() already carries the V2 row -- NO new ObservationStore method).

S-PORTSET (@property): for an N>=2-listener Service, the inbound-capture port-set (project_service_listen_ports -> AllocationSpec.service_ports, from 01-02) equals the advertise port-set (the bridge reading desired.listeners ports) -- byte-set equality, no captured port missing from the advertised set and no advertised port missing from the captured set (DELIVER obligation #1).
Mutation gate >=80% on the bridge advertise addr-source selection and the hydrate_actual map population.
steps.implementation_notes: Rust test paradigm: Tier-1 DST (in-process, default lane -- BackendDiscoveryBridge::reconcile is the pure reconciler driving port, reconcile()->(Vec<Action>,View)). UNSKIP the two S-BRIDGE scaffolds + the S-PORTSET scaffold (#[should_panic(expected="RED scaffold")]) -- replace panic bodies with real assertions. S-PORTSET is the @property case: proptest over an arbitrary non-empty NonZeroU16 listener set (N>=2) asserting set equality across the projection path (01-02) and the advertise path; @example-pin the canonical mesh row (Some(10.99.0.6)) and host row (None). Assert only on the port-exposed observable universe (emitted Backend.addr, emitted service vip, the advertised fingerprint) -- never the bridge's private fields. Depends on 01-01 (V2 row) + 01-02 (workload_addr on the row + service_ports projection). Mutation-gate the addr-source branch (Some -> workload_addr vs None -> host_ipv4) and the hydrate_actual read.

steps.name: ServiceMapHydrator GATE — three-way subnet-membership split
steps.description: Gate the ServiceMapHydrator so Path-A/mesh backends (addr in WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE) program NEITHER the cgroup LOCAL_BACKEND_MAP (no RegisterLocalBackend) NOR the XDP SERVICE_MAP/REVERSE_NAT_MAP (no DataplaneUpdateService) -- nft-TPROXY owns delivery (D-GATE / D-GATE-PRED). Adds a workload_subnet: Ipv4Net mandatory ctor param wired from WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE (one source) at the construction site. B2 (02-01) reclassifies these backends LOCAL->REMOTE, so without this gate the slice ships dead XDP writes -- the gate is the cost of B2's correctness and lands with it.
criteria: S-GATE (mesh arm, happy): a backend whose addr.ip() is within WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE (10.99.0.0/16) emits NEITHER RegisterLocalBackend NOR DataplaneUpdateService (mesh -> skip; nft-TPROXY owns delivery).
S-GATE (local arm, error/edge -- gate must NOT over-fire): a backend whose addr == host_ipv4 still emits RegisterLocalBackend (LOCAL arm UNCHANGED).
S-GATE (remote arm, error/edge -- gate must NOT over-fire): a backend whose addr is neither host_ipv4 nor within WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE still emits DataplaneUpdateService (REMOTE arm UNCHANGED).

The three-way split is applied BEFORE the existing LOCAL/REMOTE partition (mesh -> skip / local -> RegisterLocalBackend / remote -> DataplaneUpdateService); the existing partition logic for the two non-mesh arms is byte-for-byte unchanged.

ServiceMapHydrator gains workload_subnet: Ipv4Net as a MANDATORY constructor parameter (required, not defaulted, per development.md 'Port-trait dependencies'); the construction site in overdrive-control-plane passes WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE -- the SAME source the provisioner uses (one source).

Mutation gate >=80% on the hydrator partition (the three-way subnet split is a canonical mutation surface -- a mutant gating everything, gating nothing, or swapping an arm must be caught by the mesh + the two non-mesh S-GATE arms).
steps.implementation_notes: Rust test paradigm: Tier-1 DST (in-process, default lane -- ServiceMapHydrator::reconcile is the pure reconciler driving port). UNSKIP the three S-GATE scaffolds (mesh / local / remote arms) -- replace panic bodies with real assertions. PBT-eligible over the three address classes (@example-pin one representative per arm: 10.99.0.6 mesh / host_ipv4 local / 10.96.0.50 remote). Assert on the port-exposed observable universe (register_local_backend_count, dataplane_update_service_count, the programmed fingerprint) -- never the hydrator's private fields. The two non-mesh arms are the load-bearing error/edge coverage: they prove the gate does not over-fire. Per CLAUDE.md 'implement to the design': add ONLY the workload_subnet ctor param + the third (mesh) partition arm -- do NOT invent a mesh-flag field or a new predicate input; the addr's subnet membership IS the classification. Mutation gate is mandatory here (the partition is the named canonical mutation surface). Depends on 02-01 (B2 produces the workload_addr backends this gate classifies).

steps.name: start_alloc per-port inbound install (replace tproxy_guard = None)
steps.description: Close the D-A1 keystone install / D-BLOCKER1: replace start_alloc's tproxy_guard = None with one install_inbound_tproxy(SocketAddrV4::new(addr, port.get()), leg_c_addr.port()) per declared service port when spec.workload_addr is Some(addr), retaining the returned guards in AllocIntercept for the alloc lifetime. N declared ports -> N inbound rules; 0 listeners / None addr -> 0 rules. The rule match dport is the declared service port (D-BLOCKER1, one-source/two-readers), NOT the ephemeral leg-C listener port. Reuses install_inbound_tproxy AS-IS (signature unchanged) and the inline leg_c_addr local (mtls_intercept_worker.rs:585).
criteria: S-NRULES (Tier-3, real nft): a Service deployed with two declared service ports installs exactly two inbound capture rules; each rule matches ip daddr <workload_addr> tcp dport <port_i> for one of the two declared ports; both rules are released (RAII guards dropped) when the workload is torn down -- no leftover nft state after the alloc ends.

S-DPORT (Tier-3, error/edge): the installed rule's match dport is the DECLARED service port, NOT the agent's ephemeral leg-C transparent-listener port (leg_c_addr.port()); a peer dialing workload_addr:service_port is captured; the rule's tproxy-to target is the ephemeral leg-C port (the redirect destination, not the match key). The inert self-referential 'virt from ephemeral leg-C port' shape the design rejected is structurally absent.

S-JOB0 (Tier-3, error/edge): a Job-kind workload deployed with no declared service ports (empty service_ports / None workload_addr) installs ZERO inbound capture rules; no spurious all-TCP or hardcoded-port rule; no TproxyInterceptGuard retained.
AllocIntercept._tproxy_guard: Option<TproxyInterceptGuard> becomes _inbound_tproxy_guards: Vec<TproxyInterceptGuard> (N listeners -> N RAII guards, all dropped on alloc teardown).
The install is the production start_alloc call site -- no test-installed install_inbound_tproxy stands in for it.
steps.implementation_notes: Rust test paradigm: Tier-3 real-kernel (Lima, gated --features integration-tests; non-root SKIPs; record uname -r). UNSKIP the three Tier-3 scaffolds (S-NRULES / S-DPORT / S-JOB0) -- replace panic bodies with real assertions that observe the live nft ruleset (real nft dump) after the production start_alloc install. Single-example per scenario is correct for real-kernel wiring (proptest is the wrong tool for I/O). Each runs via cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests; the rule install MUST be the production start_alloc call site, never a test-installed install_inbound_tproxy (vertical-slice rule, CLAUDE.md). Per CLAUDE.md 'implement to the design': reuse install_inbound_tproxy AS-IS (signature (virt: SocketAddrV4, agent_port: u16) unchanged) and the inline leg_c_addr local -- do NOT add a new install primitive or a new arg. dport = declared service port (D-BLOCKER1), tproxy-to = ephemeral leg-C port. Depends on 01-02 (spec.workload_addr + spec.service_ports feed the loop).

steps.name: Keystone Tier-3 bidirectional mesh e2e on the production inbound rule (S-WS)
steps.description: The vertical-slice gate: a real overdrive serve + two overdrive deploy-ed mesh workloads, client dials the server's canonical workload_addr:service_port directly (no name lookup), the PRODUCTION-installed inbound rule (from start_alloc off spec.{workload_addr,service_ports}) captures the dial, mTLS terminates, request bytes reach the server and reply bytes return byte-for-byte. REMOVES the test-installed install_inbound_tproxy(virt, leg_c_port) AND the synthetic INBOUND_VIRT_IP/INBOUND_VIRT_PORT loopback virt from the existing bidirectional_walking_skeleton.rs -- no test-only wiring stands in for a production call site.
criteria: S-WS: given a node started with overdrive serve and two overdrive deploy-ed mesh workloads (a server offering a service on its declared port, a client in the same mesh), when the client dials the server at its canonical workload_addr:service_port directly with no name lookup, then the node's own PRODUCTION inbound capture (installed when the server started, from spec.{workload_addr,service_ports}) diverts the dial to the server's transparent listener; the connection is authenticated with mutual TLS; the client's request bytes arrive at the server byte-for-byte; the server's reply bytes return to the client byte-for-byte.

MERGE-BLOCKING (DELIVER obligation #3): the bidirectional mesh loop passes the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix (ADR-0068), not merely 'tests pass' -- dev-Lima 7.0 is necessary-but-not-sufficient (the built-in-ca-operator-composition cold-boot regression is the precedent for an 'expected to work on 6.18' change that did not).

The test installs NO inbound rule, supplies NO address, and stands in for NO production call site: the synthetic INBOUND_VIRT_IP/INBOUND_VIRT_PORT virt and the test-installed install_inbound_tproxy are REMOVED from the skeleton; the C3 seam supplies workload_addr, WorkloadLifecycle::project_service_listen_ports supplies service_ports, start_alloc installs the rule. If the production install were absent, the dial is NOT captured and the round-trip FAILS -- the signal the keystone exists to give.

Driven through the real operator verbs as subprocesses (overdrive serve + overdrive deploy x2), not a #[test] assembling start_alloc by hand (RCA-P1 driving-adapter requirement + vertical-slice rule); requires root + CAP_NET_ADMIN/CAP_SYS_ADMIN (non-root SKIPs); uname -r recorded.

Verification-catalogue note: S-WS is the operator-observable E-surface expectation ('a workload is reachable at its canonical address over mTLS') flagged to graduate into verification/expectations/ at DELIVER/DEVOPS (captured against the built overdrive binary) -- flagged, not built in this step.
steps.implementation_notes: Rust test paradigm: Tier-3 real-kernel keystone, single example (real serve + deploy subprocesses; proptest is the wrong tool here). UNSKIP the S-WS scaffold in the NEW canonical_address_inbound_walking_skeleton.rs and REMOVE the synthetic virt + the test-installed install_inbound_tproxy from the existing bidirectional_walking_skeleton.rs (the issue removes BOTH -- the keystone captures on the production-installed rule only). MERGE-BLOCKING signal is the pinned-6.18 appliance-kernel Tier-3 matrix (ADR-0068), NOT dev-Lima 7.0 -- name the pinned-6.18 matrix in the AC verbatim. Inner loop: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests (dev-Lima 7.0, necessary-but-not-sufficient). This is the final vertical-slice gate: it depends on ALL prior steps (01-01 V2 row, 01-02 spec plumbing, 02-01 B2 advertise, 02-02 hydrator gate, 03-01 inbound install) because a gated mesh backend must MISS LOCAL_BACKEND_MAP so the dial falls through to the production nft-TPROXY rule -- exactly what the successful capture requires. Flag S-WS for the verification/expectations/ E-surface catalogue (do NOT build the entry in this step).

validation.summary: Strong, anchor-accurate roadmap. Every load-bearing source citation verified against the actual tree (not just the design docs) and all are precise to the line. Design-fidelity, vertical-slice, sequencing, tier-assignment, rkyv-6-step, GATE-reconciliation, and deferral-hygiene checks all pass. The keystone (03-02 S-WS) genuinely closes the serve+deploy loop and REMOVES the test-installed install_inbound_tproxy + synthetic INBOUND_VIRT_IP/PORT (verified at bidirectional_walking_skeleton.rs:177-178,1742) rather than retaining a mechanism-without-wiring.
non_blocking_dispatch_refinements: 02-01 / S-PORTSET (thought): the two read paths are structurally asymmetric — project_service_listen_ports collects svc.listeners (Vec) while the bridge iterates desired.listeners as a (service_id, listener) map (backend_discovery_bridge.rs:336). The property is sound only if both bottom out in the same svc.listeners source; crafter should assert equality over a genuine N>=2-listener single Service flowing through BOTH paths so the generator can't mask a shape mismatch.

02-02 (suggestion): name the concrete partition site in the crafter dispatch — pre-filter addr.ip() in workload_subnet OUT of the backend slice BEFORE the service_map_hydrator.rs:341 .partition() call; do NOT widen the partition closure's bool into a 3-way (a malformed impl could still pass the three S-GATE arms if mesh backends leak into remote).

02-02 (question): pin the exact hydrator-construction line that passes WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE — it is the single anchor the roadmap leaves unpinned by file:line (implementation_scope lists reconciler_runtime.rs but no line). WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE lives in overdrive-control-plane veth_provisioner.rs.
validation.review_notes: effort_hours (4/6/6/5/7/8 = 36h) confirmed advisory-only, not scope-gating (CLAUDE.md No effort/time budget cuts). All out-of-scope forward-pointers cite real OPEN issues: intended-peer pinning #242, DNS daemon #243, dialable-VIP #61; ServiceBackendRow.vip UNCHANGED is correct (#61 territory), not a hidden gap. The three non-blocking refinements are worth folding into the 02-01/02-02 crafter dispatches but do NOT gate handoff to DELIVER execution.
